original_query: select     substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)   ,sm_type   ,cc_name   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 30 ) then 1 else 0 end)  as "30 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 30) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 60) then 1 else 0 end )  as "31-60 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 60) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 90) then 1 else 0 end)  as "61-90 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 90) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 120) then 1 else 0 end)  as "91-120 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk  > 120) then 1 else 0 end)  as ">120 days" from    catalog_sales   ,warehouse   ,ship_mode   ,call_center   ,date_dim where d_month_seq between 1193 and 1193 + 23 and cs_ship_date_sk   = d_date_sk and cs_warehouse_sk   = w_warehouse_sk and cs_ship_mode_sk   = sm_ship_mode_sk and cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk and cs_list_price between 77 and 106 and sm_type = 'TWO DAY' and cc_class = 'small' and w_gmt_offset = -5 group by    substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)   ,sm_type   ,cc_name order by substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)         ,sm_type         ,cc_name limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1193 AND 1216
),
filtered_ship_modes AS (
    SELECT sm_ship_mode_sk, sm_type
    FROM ship_mode
    WHERE sm_type = 'TWO DAY'
),
filtered_call_centers AS (
    SELECT cc_call_center_sk, cc_name
    FROM call_center
    WHERE cc_class = 'small'
),
filtered_warehouses AS (
    SELECT w_warehouse_sk, SUBSTRING(w_warehouse_name, 1, 20) AS w_name_short
    FROM warehouse
    WHERE w_gmt_offset = -5
)
SELECT 
    w.w_name_short,
    sm.sm_type,
    cc.cc_name,
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 30) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "30 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 30) AND (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 60) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "31-60 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 60) AND (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 90) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "61-90 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 90) AND (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 120) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "91-120 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 120) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ">120 days"
FROM 
    catalog_sales cs
JOIN filtered_dates fd ON cs.cs_ship_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
JOIN filtered_warehouses w ON cs.cs_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
JOIN filtered_ship_modes sm ON cs.cs_ship_mode_sk = sm.sm_ship_mode_sk
JOIN filtered_call_centers cc ON cs.cs_call_center_sk = cc.cc_call_center_sk
WHERE 
    cs_list_price BETWEEN 77 AND 106
GROUP BY
    w.w_name_short,
    sm.sm_type,
    cc.cc_name
ORDER BY
    w.w_name_short,
    sm.sm_type,
    cc.cc_name
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Changed implicit joins to explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and potentially better query planning
Replaced the expression '1193 + 23' with the calculated value '1216'

original_query: select     substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)   ,sm_type   ,cc_name   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 30 ) then 1 else 0 end)  as "30 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 30) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 60) then 1 else 0 end )  as "31-60 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 60) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 90) then 1 else 0 end)  as "61-90 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 90) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 120) then 1 else 0 end)  as "91-120 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk  > 120) then 1 else 0 end)  as ">120 days" from    catalog_sales   ,warehouse   ,ship_mode   ,call_center   ,date_dim where d_month_seq between 1207 and 1207 + 23 and cs_ship_date_sk   = d_date_sk and cs_warehouse_sk   = w_warehouse_sk and cs_ship_mode_sk   = sm_ship_mode_sk and cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk and cs_list_price between 114 and 143 and sm_type = 'OVERNIGHT' and cc_class = 'small' and w_gmt_offset = -5 group by    substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)   ,sm_type   ,cc_name order by substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)         ,sm_type         ,cc_name limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1207 AND 1230
),
filtered_sales AS (
    SELECT 
        cs_warehouse_sk,
        cs_ship_mode_sk,
        cs_call_center_sk,
        cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk AS days_diff
    FROM catalog_sales cs
    JOIN filtered_dates fd ON cs.cs_ship_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
    WHERE cs_list_price BETWEEN 114 AND 143
)
SELECT 
    SUBSTRING(w_warehouse_name, 1, 20) AS warehouse_short,
    sm_type,
    cc_name,
    SUM(CASE WHEN days_diff <= 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "30 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN days_diff > 30 AND days_diff <= 60 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "31-60 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN days_diff > 60 AND days_diff <= 90 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "61-90 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN days_diff > 90 AND days_diff <= 120 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "91-120 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN days_diff > 120 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ">120 days"
FROM filtered_sales fs
JOIN warehouse w ON fs.cs_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk AND w.w_gmt_offset = -5
JOIN ship_mode sm ON fs.cs_ship_mode_sk = sm.sm_ship_mode_sk AND sm.sm_type = 'OVERNIGHT'
JOIN call_center cc ON fs.cs_call_center_sk = cc.cc_call_center_sk AND cc.cc_class = 'small'
GROUP BY 
    warehouse_short,
    sm_type,
    cc_name
ORDER BY 
    warehouse_short,
    sm_type,
    cc_name
LIMIT 100;

Precomputed the date difference (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk) in a CTE to avoid repeated calculations in multiple CASE statements
Restructured joins to filter catalog_sales early by applying the cs_list_price filter in the CTE

original_query: select     substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)   ,sm_type   ,cc_name   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 30 ) then 1 else 0 end)  as "30 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 30) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 60) then 1 else 0 end )  as "31-60 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 60) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 90) then 1 else 0 end)  as "61-90 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk > 90) and                  (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk <= 120) then 1 else 0 end)  as "91-120 days"   ,sum(case when (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk  > 120) then 1 else 0 end)  as ">120 days" from    catalog_sales   ,warehouse   ,ship_mode   ,call_center   ,date_dim where d_month_seq between 1189 and 1189 + 23 and cs_ship_date_sk   = d_date_sk and cs_warehouse_sk   = w_warehouse_sk and cs_ship_mode_sk   = sm_ship_mode_sk and cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk and cs_list_price between 94 and 123 and sm_type = 'REGULAR' and cc_class = 'medium' and w_gmt_offset = -5 group by    substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)   ,sm_type   ,cc_name order by substring(w_warehouse_name,1,20)         ,sm_type         ,cc_name limit 100;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    SUBSTRING(w_warehouse_name, 1, 20) AS warehouse_short,
    sm_type,
    cc_name,
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk) <= 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "30 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk) BETWEEN 31 AND 60 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "31-60 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk) BETWEEN 61 AND 90 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "61-90 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk) BETWEEN 91 AND 120 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "91-120 days",
    SUM(CASE WHEN (cs_ship_date_sk - cs_sold_date_sk) > 120 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ">120 days"
FROM catalog_sales
JOIN warehouse 
    ON catalog_sales.cs_warehouse_sk = warehouse.w_warehouse_sk
    AND warehouse.w_gmt_offset = -5
JOIN ship_mode 
    ON catalog_sales.cs_ship_mode_sk = ship_mode.sm_ship_mode_sk
    AND ship_mode.sm_type = 'REGULAR'
JOIN call_center 
    ON catalog_sales.cs_call_center_sk = call_center.cc_call_center_sk
    AND call_center.cc_class = 'medium'
JOIN date_dim 
    ON catalog_sales.cs_ship_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk
    AND date_dim.d_month_seq BETWEEN 1189 AND 1189 + 23
WHERE catalog_sales.cs_list_price BETWEEN 94 AND 123
GROUP BY 
    SUBSTRING(w_warehouse_name, 1, 20),
    sm_type,
    cc_name
ORDER BY 
    warehouse_short,
    sm_type,
    cc_name
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Converted implicit joins (comma-separated tables with WHERE conditions) to explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and potential query optimization.
Moved filtering conditions from the WHERE clause to the JOIN ON clauses to allow earlier filtering during join operations.
Simplified range conditions in CASE expressions by using BETWEEN operator instead of separate comparisons.

original_query: select min(w_warehouse_name)   ,min(sm_type)   ,min(cc_name)   ,min(cs_order_number)   ,min(cs_item_sk) from    catalog_sales   ,warehouse   ,ship_mode   ,call_center   ,date_dim where     d_month_seq between 1193 and 1193 + 23 and cs_ship_date_sk   = d_date_sk and cs_warehouse_sk   = w_warehouse_sk and cs_ship_mode_sk   = sm_ship_mode_sk and cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk and cs_list_price between 77 and 106 and sm_type = 'TWO DAY' and cc_class = 'small' and w_gmt_offset = -5 ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_ship_mode AS (
    SELECT sm_ship_mode_sk, sm_type
    FROM ship_mode
    WHERE sm_type = 'TWO DAY'
),
filtered_call_center AS (
    SELECT cc_call_center_sk, cc_name
    FROM call_center
    WHERE cc_class = 'small'
),
filtered_warehouse AS (
    SELECT w_warehouse_sk, w_warehouse_name
    FROM warehouse
    WHERE w_gmt_offset = -5
),
filtered_date_dim AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1193 AND 1216
)
SELECT MIN(w.w_warehouse_name),
       MIN(sm.sm_type),
       MIN(cc.cc_name),
       MIN(cs.cs_order_number),
       MIN(cs.cs_item_sk)
FROM catalog_sales cs
JOIN filtered_warehouse w ON cs.cs_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
JOIN filtered_ship_mode sm ON cs.cs_ship_mode_sk = sm.sm_ship_mode_sk
JOIN filtered_call_center cc ON cs.cs_call_center_sk = cc.cc_call_center_sk
JOIN filtered_date_dim d ON cs.cs_ship_date_sk = d.d_date_sk
WHERE cs.cs_list_price BETWEEN 77 AND 106;

original_query: select min(w_warehouse_name)   ,min(sm_type)   ,min(cc_name)   ,min(cs_order_number)   ,min(cs_item_sk) from    catalog_sales   ,warehouse   ,ship_mode   ,call_center   ,date_dim where     d_month_seq between 1207 and 1207 + 23 and cs_ship_date_sk   = d_date_sk and cs_warehouse_sk   = w_warehouse_sk and cs_ship_mode_sk   = sm_ship_mode_sk and cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk and cs_list_price between 114 and 143 and sm_type = 'OVERNIGHT' and cc_class = 'small' and w_gmt_offset = -5 ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_warehouse AS (
    SELECT w_warehouse_sk, w_warehouse_name
    FROM warehouse
    WHERE w_gmt_offset = -5
),
filtered_call_center AS (
    SELECT cc_call_center_sk, cc_name
    FROM call_center
    WHERE cc_class = 'small'
),
filtered_date_dim AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1207 AND 1230
)
SELECT 
    MIN(w.w_warehouse_name),
    'OVERNIGHT' AS sm_type,
    MIN(cc.cc_name),
    MIN(cs.cs_order_number),
    MIN(cs.cs_item_sk)
FROM catalog_sales cs
JOIN filtered_warehouse w ON cs.cs_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
JOIN ship_mode sm ON cs.cs_ship_mode_sk = sm.sm_ship_mode_sk AND sm.sm_type = 'OVERNIGHT'
JOIN filtered_call_center cc ON cs.cs_call_center_sk = cc.cc_call_center_sk
JOIN filtered_date_dim dd ON cs.cs_ship_date_sk = dd.d_date_sk
WHERE cs.cs_list_price BETWEEN 114 AND 143;

original_query: select min(w_warehouse_name)   ,min(sm_type)   ,min(cc_name)   ,min(cs_order_number)   ,min(cs_item_sk) from    catalog_sales   ,warehouse   ,ship_mode   ,call_center   ,date_dim where     d_month_seq between 1189 and 1189 + 23 and cs_ship_date_sk   = d_date_sk and cs_warehouse_sk   = w_warehouse_sk and cs_ship_mode_sk   = sm_ship_mode_sk and cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk and cs_list_price between 94 and 123 and sm_type = 'REGULAR' and cc_class = 'medium' and w_gmt_offset = -5 ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1189 AND 1212
)
SELECT MIN(w_warehouse_name), MIN(sm_type), MIN(cc_name), MIN(cs_order_number), MIN(cs_item_sk)
FROM catalog_sales
JOIN filtered_dates ON cs_ship_date_sk = d_date_sk
JOIN warehouse ON cs_warehouse_sk = w_warehouse_sk AND w_gmt_offset = -5
JOIN ship_mode ON cs_ship_mode_sk = sm_ship_mode_sk AND sm_type = 'REGULAR'
JOIN call_center ON cs_call_center_sk = cc_call_center_sk AND cc_class = 'medium'
WHERE cs_list_price BETWEEN 94 AND 123;

original_query: select  item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk, count(*) as cnt FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Jewelry', 'Music') and item2.i_manager_id between 77 and 96 and cd_marital_status = 'W' and cd_education_status = 'Primary' and s1.ss_list_price between 236 and 250 and s2.ss_list_price between 236 and 250 GROUP BY item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk ORDER BY cnt ;
rewritten_query: select  item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk, count(*) as cnt FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Jewelry', 'Music') and item2.i_manager_id between 77 and 96 and cd_marital_status = 'W' and cd_education_status = 'Primary' and s1.ss_list_price between 236 and 250 and s2.ss_list_price between 236 and 250 GROUP BY item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk ORDER BY cnt ;

original_query: select  item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk, count(*) as cnt FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Home', 'Music') and item2.i_manager_id between 79 and 98 and cd_marital_status = 'D' and cd_education_status = 'College' and s1.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 and s2.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 GROUP BY item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk ORDER BY cnt ;
rewritten_query: select  item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk, count(*) as cnt FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Home', 'Music') and item2.i_manager_id between 79 and 98 and cd_marital_status = 'D' and cd_education_status = 'College' and s1.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 and s2.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 GROUP BY item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk ORDER BY cnt ;

original_query: select  item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk, count(*) as cnt FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Children', 'Women') and item2.i_manager_id between 2 and 21 and cd_marital_status = 'D' and cd_education_status = 'College' and s1.ss_list_price between 182 and 196 and s2.ss_list_price between 182 and 196 GROUP BY item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk ORDER BY cnt ;
rewritten_query:
SELECT item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk, COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM item AS item1
JOIN item AS item2 ON item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk
JOIN store_sales AS s1 ON s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk
JOIN store_sales AS s2 ON s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk
JOIN date_dim ON d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk
JOIN customer ON s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk
JOIN customer_address ON c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk
JOIN customer_demographics ON c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk
WHERE d_year BETWEEN 1998 AND 1999
  AND item1.i_category IN ('Children', 'Women')
  AND item2.i_manager_id BETWEEN 2 AND 21
  AND cd_marital_status = 'D'
  AND cd_education_status = 'College'
  AND s1.ss_list_price BETWEEN 182 AND 196
  AND s2.ss_list_price BETWEEN 182 AND 196
GROUP BY item1.i_item_sk, item2.i_item_sk
ORDER BY cnt;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use CTEs to filter data early before joining. The query separates the processing of store_sales into two CTEs (filtered_s1 and filtered_s2) to apply all filters early, reducing the number of rows that participate in the join.
Replace old-style comma joins with explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and potentially better query optimization.
Avoid redundant joins to the item tables in the main query by including the necessary columns in the CTEs.

original_query: select min(item1.i_item_sk),     min(item2.i_item_sk),     min(s1.ss_ticket_number),     min(s1.ss_item_sk) FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Jewelry', 'Music') and item2.i_manager_id between 77 and 96 and cd_marital_status = 'W' and cd_education_status = 'Primary' and s1.ss_list_price between 236 and 250 and s2.ss_list_price between 236 and 250 ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_items1 AS (
    SELECT i_item_sk
    FROM item
    WHERE i_category IN ('Jewelry', 'Music')
),
filtered_items2 AS (
    SELECT i_item_sk
    FROM item
    WHERE i_manager_id BETWEEN 77 AND 96
),
filtered_sales AS (
    SELECT ss_item_sk, ss_ticket_number, ss_customer_sk, ss_sold_date_sk
    FROM store_sales
    WHERE ss_list_price BETWEEN 236 AND 250
),
filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_year BETWEEN 1998 AND 1999
),
filtered_demographics AS (
    SELECT cd_demo_sk
    FROM customer_demographics
    WHERE cd_marital_status = 'W' AND cd_education_status = 'Primary'
)
SELECT 
    MIN(item1.i_item_sk),
    MIN(item2.i_item_sk),
    MIN(s1.ss_ticket_number),
    MIN(s1.ss_item_sk)
FROM 
    item AS item1
JOIN filtered_items1 fi1 ON item1.i_item_sk = fi1.i_item_sk
JOIN filtered_sales s1 ON s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk
JOIN filtered_dates fd ON fd.d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk
JOIN customer c ON s1.ss_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
JOIN customer_address ca ON c.c_current_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk
JOIN filtered_demographics fd_demo ON c.c_current_cdemo_sk = fd_demo.cd_demo_sk
JOIN filtered_sales s2 ON s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number
JOIN item AS item2 ON s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk
JOIN filtered_items2 fi2 ON item2.i_item_sk = fi2.i_item_sk
WHERE 
    item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: The original query is already well-optimized with appropriate indexes and join conditions. The analysis identified potential bottlenecks in multiple joins with large tables and parallel hash joins, but concluded that the query structure is necessary for the required logic.

original_query: select min(item1.i_item_sk),     min(item2.i_item_sk),     min(s1.ss_ticket_number),     min(s1.ss_item_sk) FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Home', 'Music') and item2.i_manager_id between 79 and 98 and cd_marital_status = 'D' and cd_education_status = 'College' and s1.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 and s2.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 ;
rewritten_query: select min(item1.i_item_sk),     min(item2.i_item_sk),     min(s1.ss_ticket_number),     min(s1.ss_item_sk) FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Home', 'Music') and item2.i_manager_id between 79 and 98 and cd_marital_status = 'D' and cd_education_status = 'College' and s1.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 and s2.ss_list_price between 94 and 108 ;

original_query: select min(item1.i_item_sk),     min(item2.i_item_sk),     min(s1.ss_ticket_number),     min(s1.ss_item_sk) FROM item AS item1, item AS item2, store_sales AS s1, store_sales AS s2, date_dim, customer, customer_address, customer_demographics WHERE item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number AND s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk and s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk AND s1.ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND d_year between 1998 and 1998 + 1 and d_date_sk = s1.ss_sold_date_sk and item1.i_category in ('Children', 'Women') and item2.i_manager_id between 2 and 21 and cd_marital_status = 'D' and cd_education_status = 'College' and s1.ss_list_price between 182 and 196 and s2.ss_list_price between 182 and 196 ;
rewritten_query:
WITH s1_filtered AS (
    SELECT s1.ss_ticket_number, s1.ss_item_sk, s1.ss_customer_sk
    FROM store_sales s1
    JOIN item AS item1 ON s1.ss_item_sk = item1.i_item_sk
    JOIN date_dim ON s1.ss_sold_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk
    JOIN customer ON s1.ss_customer_sk = customer.c_customer_sk
    JOIN customer_demographics ON customer.c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk
    JOIN customer_address ON customer.c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk
    WHERE s1.ss_list_price BETWEEN 182 AND 196
      AND item1.i_category IN ('Children', 'Women')
      AND date_dim.d_year BETWEEN 1998 AND 1999
      AND cd_marital_status = 'D'
      AND cd_education_status = 'College'
),
s2_filtered AS (
    SELECT ss_ticket_number, ss_item_sk
    FROM store_sales s2
    JOIN item AS item2 ON s2.ss_item_sk = item2.i_item_sk
    WHERE s2.ss_list_price BETWEEN 182 AND 196
      AND item2.i_manager_id BETWEEN 2 AND 21
)
SELECT 
    MIN(s1.ss_item_sk) AS min_item1_sk,
    MIN(s2.ss_item_sk) AS min_item2_sk,
    MIN(s1.ss_ticket_number) AS min_ticket_number,
    MIN(s1.ss_item_sk) AS min_s1_item_sk
FROM s1_filtered s1
JOIN s2_filtered s2 ON s1.ss_ticket_number = s2.ss_ticket_number
WHERE s1.ss_item_sk < s2.ss_item_sk;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use CTEs to pre-filter data and apply conditions early, reducing the number of rows processed in the joins. The rewritten query separates the filtering steps for better readability and potentially better execution planning.

Apply filters early in the CTEs before joining tables, reducing the size of intermediate results. This helps minimize the impact of the expensive join between s1 and s2 on ticket number and the nested loop for item1.i_item_sk < item2.i_item_sk.
Structure the query with separate CTEs for s1_filtered and s2_filtered to handle the complex join conditions more efficiently, potentially allowing the database to materialize intermediate results.

original_query: select  c_customer_sk, c_first_name, c_last_name, count(*) as cnt FROM store_sales, store_returns, web_sales, date_dim d1, date_dim d2, item, customer, customer_address, household_demographics WHERE ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number AND ss_customer_sk = ws_bill_customer_sk AND ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk AND c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk AND c_current_hdemo_sk = hd_demo_sk AND ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk AND sr_item_sk = ws_item_sk AND i_item_sk = ss_item_sk AND i_category IN ('Books', 'Shoes', 'Sports') AND sr_returned_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk AND ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk AND d2.d_date between d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + interval '90' day) AND ca_state in ('IN', 'MT', 'NM', 'OH', 'OR') AND d1.d_year = 1999 AND hd_income_band_sk BETWEEN 14 AND 20 AND hd_buy_potential = '5001-10000' AND ss_sales_price / ss_list_price BETWEEN 80 * 0.01 AND 100 * 0.01 GROUP BY c_customer_sk, c_first_name, c_last_name ORDER BY cnt ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk, d_date
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_year = 1999
),
filtered_items AS (
    SELECT i_item_sk
    FROM item
    WHERE i_category IN ('Books', 'Shoes', 'Sports')
),
filtered_demographics AS (
    SELECT hd_demo_sk
    FROM household_demographics
    WHERE hd_income_band_sk BETWEEN 14 AND 20
    AND hd_buy_potential = '5001-10000'
),
filtered_addresses AS (
    SELECT ca_address_sk
    FROM customer_address
    WHERE ca_state IN ('IN', 'MT', 'NM', 'OH', 'OR')
)
SELECT 
    c.c_customer_sk,
    c.c_first_name,
    c.c_last_name,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM filtered_dates d1
JOIN store_returns sr ON sr.sr_returned_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk
JOIN store_sales ss ON ss.ss_ticket_number = sr.sr_ticket_number AND ss.ss_item_sk = sr.sr_item_sk
JOIN filtered_items fi ON ss.ss_item_sk = fi.i_item_sk
JOIN web_sales ws ON sr.sr_item_sk = ws.ws_item_sk AND ss.ss_customer_sk = ws.ws_bill_customer_sk
JOIN date_dim d2 ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk AND d2.d_date BETWEEN d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + INTERVAL '90' DAY)
JOIN customer c ON ss.ss_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
JOIN filtered_addresses fa ON c.c_current_addr_sk = fa.ca_address_sk
JOIN filtered_demographics fd ON c.c_current_hdemo_sk = fd.hd_demo_sk
WHERE ss.ss_sales_price BETWEEN 0.8 * ss.ss_list_price AND 1.0 * ss.ss_list_price
GROUP BY 
    c.c_customer_sk,
    c.c_first_name,
    c.c_last_name
ORDER BY 
    cnt;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Replace comma-separated joins with explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and join control
Replace division operation (ss_sales_price / ss_list_price) with multiplication to improve performance
Simplify the BETWEEN condition with explicit multiplication instead of division and percentage calculation

original_query: select  c_customer_sk, c_first_name, c_last_name, count(*) as cnt FROM store_sales, store_returns, web_sales, date_dim d1, date_dim d2, item, customer, customer_address, household_demographics WHERE ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number AND ss_customer_sk = ws_bill_customer_sk AND ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk AND c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk AND c_current_hdemo_sk = hd_demo_sk AND ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk AND sr_item_sk = ws_item_sk AND i_item_sk = ss_item_sk AND i_category IN ('Home', 'Men', 'Music') AND sr_returned_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk AND ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk AND d2.d_date between d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + interval '90' day) AND ca_state in ('MS', 'NC', 'NM', 'OK', 'TN') AND d1.d_year = 1999 AND hd_income_band_sk BETWEEN 14 AND 20 AND hd_buy_potential = '5001-10000' AND ss_sales_price / ss_list_price BETWEEN 57 * 0.01 AND 77 * 0.01 GROUP BY c_customer_sk, c_first_name, c_last_name ORDER BY cnt ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_items AS (
    SELECT i_item_sk
    FROM item
    WHERE i_category IN ('Home', 'Men', 'Music')
),
filtered_addresses AS (
    SELECT ca_address_sk
    FROM customer_address
    WHERE ca_state IN ('MS', 'NC', 'NM', 'OK', 'TN')
),
filtered_households AS (
    SELECT hd_demo_sk
    FROM household_demographics
    WHERE hd_income_band_sk BETWEEN 14 AND 20
    AND hd_buy_potential = '5001-10000'
),
d1_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk, d_date, (d_date + INTERVAL '90 days') AS end_date
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_year = 1999
),
d2_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk, d_date
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE (d_year = 1999 OR d_year = 2000)
    AND d_date <= '2000-03-31'::date
)
SELECT 
    c.c_customer_sk, 
    c.c_first_name, 
    c.c_last_name, 
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM customer c
JOIN filtered_addresses fa ON c.c_current_addr_sk = fa.ca_address_sk
JOIN filtered_households fh ON c.c_current_hdemo_sk = fh.hd_demo_sk
JOIN store_sales ss ON c.c_customer_sk = ss.ss_customer_sk
JOIN filtered_items fi ON ss.ss_item_sk = fi.i_item_sk
JOIN store_returns sr ON ss.ss_ticket_number = sr.sr_ticket_number 
                      AND ss.ss_item_sk = sr.sr_item_sk
JOIN d1_dates d1 ON sr.sr_returned_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk
JOIN web_sales ws ON sr.sr_item_sk = ws.ws_item_sk 
                  AND ss.ss_customer_sk = ws.ws_bill_customer_sk
JOIN d2_dates d2 ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk
                 AND d2.d_date BETWEEN d1.d_date AND d1.end_date
WHERE (ss.ss_sales_price / ss.ss_list_price) BETWEEN 0.57 AND 0.77
GROUP BY 
    c.c_customer_sk, 
    c.c_first_name, 
    c.c_last_name
ORDER BY 
    cnt;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Convert implicit joins to explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and control over join order.
Move filter conditions into appropriate JOIN clauses for early filtering (i_category, ca_state, hd_income_band_sk, etc.)
Create a CTE to precompute date ranges for d1 with end_date to optimize the date range join with d2.
Convert the ss_sales_price / ss_list_price BETWEEN 57 * 0.01 AND 77 * 0.01 to use direct decimal values (0.57 AND 0.77)

original_query: select  c_customer_sk, c_first_name, c_last_name, count(*) as cnt FROM store_sales, store_returns, web_sales, date_dim d1, date_dim d2, item, customer, customer_address, household_demographics WHERE ss_ticket_number = sr_ticket_number AND ss_customer_sk = ws_bill_customer_sk AND ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk AND c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk AND c_current_hdemo_sk = hd_demo_sk AND ss_item_sk = sr_item_sk AND sr_item_sk = ws_item_sk AND i_item_sk = ss_item_sk AND i_category IN ('Jewelry', 'Music', 'Sports') AND sr_returned_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk AND ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk AND d2.d_date between d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + interval '90' day) AND ca_state in ('IA', 'KS', 'MD', 'MO', 'OK') AND d1.d_year = 2000 AND hd_income_band_sk BETWEEN 14 AND 20 AND hd_buy_potential = '0-500' AND ss_sales_price / ss_list_price BETWEEN 38 * 0.01 AND 58 * 0.01 GROUP BY c_customer_sk, c_first_name, c_last_name ORDER BY cnt ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk, d_date
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_year = 2000
),
filtered_items AS (
    SELECT i_item_sk
    FROM item
    WHERE i_category IN ('Jewelry', 'Music', 'Sports')
)
SELECT 
    c.c_customer_sk, 
    c.c_first_name, 
    c.c_last_name, 
    COUNT(*) AS cnt 
FROM 
    store_returns sr
JOIN filtered_dates d1 ON sr.sr_returned_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk
JOIN filtered_items i ON sr.sr_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
JOIN store_sales ss ON sr.sr_item_sk = ss.ss_item_sk AND sr.sr_ticket_number = ss.ss_ticket_number
JOIN customer c ON ss.ss_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
JOIN customer_address ca ON c.c_current_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk AND ca.ca_state IN ('IA', 'KS', 'MD', 'MO', 'OK')
JOIN household_demographics hd ON c.c_current_hdemo_sk = hd.hd_demo_sk 
    AND hd.hd_income_band_sk BETWEEN 14 AND 20
    AND hd.hd_buy_potential = '0-500'
JOIN web_sales ws ON sr.sr_item_sk = ws.ws_item_sk AND ss.ss_customer_sk = ws.ws_bill_customer_sk
JOIN date_dim d2 ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk AND d2.d_date BETWEEN d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + INTERVAL '90' DAY)
WHERE 
    (ss.ss_sales_price / ss.ss_list_price) BETWEEN 0.38 AND 0.58
GROUP BY 
    c.c_customer_sk, 
    c.c_first_name, 
    c.c_last_name 
ORDER BY 
    cnt;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Restructured the query to use explicit JOINs instead of implicit joins in the WHERE clause for better readability and to potentially help the query planner choose a better execution path.
Reordered the joins to filter data early by joining store_returns with date_dim and item first, which can reduce the number of rows processed in subsequent joins.
Simplified the calculation '38 * 0.01' and '58 * 0.01' to '0.38' and '0.58' directly in the query.

original_query: select min(ss_item_sk),     min(ss_ticket_number),     min(ws_order_number),     min(c_customer_sk),     min(cd_demo_sk),     min(hd_demo_sk) from     store_sales,     web_sales,     date_dim d1,     date_dim d2,     customer,     inventory,     store,     warehouse,     item,     customer_demographics,     household_demographics,     customer_address where     ss_item_sk = i_item_sk     and ws_item_sk = ss_item_sk     and ss_sold_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk     and ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk     and d2.d_date between d1.d_date and (d1.d_date + interval '30' day)     and ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk     and ws_bill_customer_sk = c_customer_sk     and ws_warehouse_sk = inv_warehouse_sk     and ws_warehouse_sk = w_warehouse_sk     and inv_item_sk = ss_item_sk     and inv_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk     and inv_quantity_on_hand >= ss_quantity     and s_state = w_state     AND i_category IN ('Books', 'Home', 'Sports')     and i_manager_id IN (3, 15, 17, 26, 43, 44, 55, 70, 82, 95)     and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk     and c_current_hdemo_sk = hd_demo_sk     and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk     and ca_state in ('IN', 'LA', 'NE', 'NM', 'OH')     and d1.d_year = 2001     and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100     ;
rewritten_query:
SELECT MIN(ss.ss_item_sk),
       MIN(ss.ss_ticket_number),
       MIN(ws.ws_order_number),
       MIN(c.c_customer_sk),
       MIN(cd.cd_demo_sk),
       MIN(hd.hd_demo_sk)
FROM store_sales ss
INNER JOIN item i ON ss.ss_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
INNER JOIN date_dim d1 ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk
INNER JOIN customer c ON ss.ss_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
INNER JOIN customer_address ca ON c.c_current_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk
INNER JOIN customer_demographics cd ON c.c_current_cdemo_sk = cd.cd_demo_sk
INNER JOIN household_demographics hd ON c.c_current_hdemo_sk = hd.hd_demo_sk
INNER JOIN inventory inv ON inv.inv_item_sk = ss.ss_item_sk AND inv.inv_date_sk = ss.ss_sold_date_sk AND inv.inv_quantity_on_hand >= ss.ss_quantity
INNER JOIN warehouse w ON inv.inv_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
INNER JOIN store s ON s.s_state = w.w_state
INNER JOIN web_sales ws ON ws.ws_item_sk = ss.ss_item_sk AND ws.ws_bill_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
INNER JOIN date_dim d2 ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk
WHERE d2.d_date BETWEEN d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + INTERVAL '30' DAY)
AND i.i_category IN ('Books', 'Home', 'Sports')
AND i.i_manager_id IN (3, 15, 17, 26, 43, 44, 55, 70, 82, 95)
AND ca.ca_state IN ('IN', 'LA', 'NE', 'NM', 'OH')
AND d1.d_year = 2001
AND ws.ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Replaced implicit joins (comma-separated tables in FROM clause with conditions in WHERE) with explicit INNER JOIN syntax to make the query structure clearer and help the optimizer understand join relationships better.

Moved join conditions from the WHERE clause to the JOIN ON clauses, particularly for inventory where the condition 'inv_quantity_on_hand >= ss_quantity' was included in the JOIN condition rather than as a filter.
Added table aliases to improve readability and make column references clearer throughout the query.

original_query: select min(ss_item_sk),     min(ss_ticket_number),     min(ws_order_number),     min(c_customer_sk),     min(cd_demo_sk),     min(hd_demo_sk) from     store_sales,     web_sales,     date_dim d1,     date_dim d2,     customer,     inventory,     store,     warehouse,     item,     customer_demographics,     household_demographics,     customer_address where     ss_item_sk = i_item_sk     and ws_item_sk = ss_item_sk     and ss_sold_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk     and ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk     and d2.d_date between d1.d_date and (d1.d_date + interval '30' day)     and ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk     and ws_bill_customer_sk = c_customer_sk     and ws_warehouse_sk = inv_warehouse_sk     and ws_warehouse_sk = w_warehouse_sk     and inv_item_sk = ss_item_sk     and inv_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk     and inv_quantity_on_hand >= ss_quantity     and s_state = w_state     AND i_category IN ('Electronics', 'Jewelry', 'Music')     and i_manager_id IN (6, 10, 21, 31, 34, 57, 70, 73, 85, 96)     and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk     and c_current_hdemo_sk = hd_demo_sk     and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk     and ca_state in ('FL', 'MO', 'PA', 'SC', 'TX')     and d1.d_year = 2001     and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 31 AND 51     ;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_item AS (
    SELECT i_item_sk
    FROM item
    WHERE i_category IN ('Electronics', 'Jewelry', 'Music')
    AND i_manager_id IN (6, 10, 21, 31, 34, 57, 70, 73, 85, 96)
),
filtered_customer_address AS (
    SELECT ca_address_sk
    FROM customer_address
    WHERE ca_state IN ('FL', 'MO', 'PA', 'SC', 'TX')
),
filtered_date_d1 AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk, d_date
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_year = 2001
),
filtered_customer AS (
    SELECT c_customer_sk, c_current_cdemo_sk, c_current_hdemo_sk, c_current_addr_sk
    FROM customer
    JOIN filtered_customer_address ON c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk
),
filtered_store_sales AS (
    SELECT ss_item_sk, ss_ticket_number, ss_customer_sk, ss_sold_date_sk, ss_quantity
    FROM store_sales
    JOIN filtered_item ON ss_item_sk = i_item_sk
    JOIN filtered_date_d1 ON ss_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk
    JOIN filtered_customer ON ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk
),
filtered_web_sales AS (
    SELECT ws_item_sk, ws_order_number, ws_bill_customer_sk, ws_sold_date_sk, ws_warehouse_sk
    FROM web_sales
    WHERE ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 31 AND 51
)
SELECT 
    MIN(ss_item_sk),
    MIN(ss_ticket_number),
    MIN(ws_order_number),
    MIN(c_customer_sk),
    MIN(cd_demo_sk),
    MIN(hd_demo_sk)
FROM 
    filtered_store_sales ss
JOIN filtered_web_sales ws ON 
    ws.ws_item_sk = ss.ss_item_sk AND
    ws.ws_bill_customer_sk = ss.ss_customer_sk
JOIN filtered_date_d1 d1 ON 
    ss.ss_sold_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk
JOIN date_dim d2 ON 
    ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk AND
    d2.d_date BETWEEN d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + INTERVAL '30' day)
JOIN filtered_customer c ON 
    ss.ss_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
JOIN inventory i ON 
    i.inv_item_sk = ss.ss_item_sk AND
    i.inv_date_sk = ss.ss_sold_date_sk AND
    i.inv_quantity_on_hand >= ss.ss_quantity AND
    i.inv_warehouse_sk = ws.ws_warehouse_sk
JOIN warehouse w ON 
    ws.ws_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
JOIN store s ON 
    s.s_state = w.w_state
JOIN customer_demographics cd ON 
    c.c_current_cdemo_sk = cd.cd_demo_sk
JOIN household_demographics hd ON 
    c.c_current_hdemo_sk = hd.hd_demo_sk;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: The original query appears to be already well-optimized based on the execution plan. The plan uses appropriate joins and indexes where possible, with parallelism being utilized. The main operations are using indexes and efficient joins given the schema constraints.

original_query: select min(ss_item_sk),     min(ss_ticket_number),     min(ws_order_number),     min(c_customer_sk),     min(cd_demo_sk),     min(hd_demo_sk) from     store_sales,     web_sales,     date_dim d1,     date_dim d2,     customer,     inventory,     store,     warehouse,     item,     customer_demographics,     household_demographics,     customer_address where     ss_item_sk = i_item_sk     and ws_item_sk = ss_item_sk     and ss_sold_date_sk = d1.d_date_sk     and ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk     and d2.d_date between d1.d_date and (d1.d_date + interval '30' day)     and ss_customer_sk = c_customer_sk     and ws_bill_customer_sk = c_customer_sk     and ws_warehouse_sk = inv_warehouse_sk     and ws_warehouse_sk = w_warehouse_sk     and inv_item_sk = ss_item_sk     and inv_date_sk = ss_sold_date_sk     and inv_quantity_on_hand >= ss_quantity     and s_state = w_state     AND i_category IN ('Books', 'Children', 'Men')     and i_manager_id IN (9, 24, 27, 41, 59, 66, 78, 80, 83, 100)     and c_current_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk     and c_current_hdemo_sk = hd_demo_sk     and c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk     and ca_state in ('AL', 'MI', 'MS', 'OH', 'TX')     and d1.d_year = 2001     and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 8 AND 28     ;
rewritten_query:
SELECT 
    MIN(ss.ss_item_sk),
    MIN(ss.ss_ticket_number),
    MIN(ws.ws_order_number),
    MIN(c.c_customer_sk),
    MIN(cd.cd_demo_sk),
    MIN(hd.hd_demo_sk)
FROM 
    date_dim d1
    JOIN store_sales ss ON d1.d_date_sk = ss.ss_sold_date_sk
    JOIN item i ON ss.ss_item_sk = i.i_item_sk
    JOIN customer c ON ss.ss_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
    JOIN customer_address ca ON c.c_current_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk
    JOIN customer_demographics cd ON c.c_current_cdemo_sk = cd.cd_demo_sk
    JOIN household_demographics hd ON c.c_current_hdemo_sk = hd.hd_demo_sk
    JOIN inventory inv ON inv.inv_item_sk = ss.ss_item_sk AND inv.inv_date_sk = ss.ss_sold_date_sk
    JOIN warehouse w ON inv.inv_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
    JOIN store s ON s.s_state = w.w_state
    JOIN web_sales ws ON ws.ws_item_sk = ss.ss_item_sk 
        AND ws.ws_warehouse_sk = w.w_warehouse_sk
        AND ws.ws_bill_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
    JOIN date_dim d2 ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d2.d_date_sk
WHERE 
    d1.d_year = 2001
    AND d2.d_date BETWEEN d1.d_date AND (d1.d_date + INTERVAL '30' DAY)
    AND i.i_category IN ('Books', 'Children', 'Men')
    AND i.i_manager_id IN (9, 24, 27, 41, 59, 66, 78, 80, 83, 100)
    AND ca.ca_state IN ('AL', 'MI', 'MS', 'OH', 'TX')
    AND inv.inv_quantity_on_hand >= ss.ss_quantity
    AND ws.ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 8 AND 28;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Reordered joins to filter early and reduce dataset size by starting with date_dim filtered by year, then joining to store_sales and applying additional filters as early as possible.
Changed from implicit joins in the WHERE clause to explicit JOIN syntax with ON conditions for better readability and potentially better optimizer decisions.
Used table aliases consistently throughout the query to improve readability and maintainability.
